title: Hover security-probe seed
description: A worked recipe that teaches the probe engine how to probe a CAPTURED REQUEST for an access-control or vulnerability bug class. Matched against captured MITM traffic, NOT against UI steps — a separate schema from the optimization (UI-translation) seed. Built-in recipes live as JSON files in packages/probe-engine/seeds/ and are inlined into @hover-dev/security / @hover-dev/pentest at build; users add their own under <projectRoot>/.hover/rules/.

category.description: Which mode draws this seed: `authz` (business / access-control — orange security mode) or `vuln` (attack / exploit — red pentest mode). Security mode pulls the authz set; pentest mode pulls everything. Defaults to authz when absent.

note.description: One-line description of the pattern and when it applies.

match.description: Cheap relevance filter over a captured request — used only to pick recipes, never exact-matched.

method.description: HTTP methods this recipe applies to.

urlParam.description: Regex over the request URL / query string.

bodyField.description: Regex over a JSON request-body key.

needsAuth.description: Only consider requests that carry a session/auth credential.

strategy.description: Human-readable probing strategy the agent follows against the captured request. Keep it IN-BAND — no out-of-band callbacks, no destructive writes unless `destructive` is declared and the user opts in.

secondIdentity.description: Requires a second captured identity (B) — replay identity A's request as B.

destructive.description: Whether the probe issues state-changing/destructive requests. The recipe only DECLARES this; the engine ENFORCES it — destructive probing is off by default and requires explicit user opt-in.

signal.description: What response differential indicates a real finding.

assert.description: Optional Playwright request-fixture assertion for a crystallized `.security.spec.ts` regression test. Must not bake real cookies/tokens/PII inline — pull credentials from env/fixture.
